title: GEMM-GS: Accelerating 3D Gaussian Splatting on Tensor Cores with GEMM-Compatible Blending
comment: Accepted by the 63rd Design Automation Conference (DAC 2026)
summary: Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) enables 3D scene reconstruction from several 2D images but incurs high rendering latency via its point-sampling design. 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) improves on NeRF with explicit scene representation and an optimized pipeline yet still fails to meet practical real-time demands. Existing acceleration works overlook the evolving Tensor Cores of modern GPUs because 3DGS pipeline lacks General Matrix Multiplication (GEMM) operations. This paper proposes GEMM-GS, an acceleration approach utilizing tensor cores on GPUs via GEMM-friendly blending transformation. It equivalently reformulates the 3DGS blending process into a GEMM-compatible form to utilize Tensor Cores. A high-performance CUDA kernel is designed, integrating a three-stage double-buffered pipeline that overlaps computation and memory access. Extensive experiments show that GEMM-GS achieves $1.42\times$ speedup over vanilla 3DGS and provides an additional $1.47\times$ speedup on average when combining with existing acceleration approaches. Code is released at https://github.com/shieldforever/GEMM-GS.

AI.tldr: GEMM-GS通过将3D高斯泼溅的混合过程重新表述为GEMM兼容形式，利用GPU张量核心实现加速，比原始3DGS快1.42倍
AI.motivation: 现有3D高斯泼溅（3DGS）加速方法忽略了现代GPU的张量核心，因为3DGS流程缺乏通用矩阵乘法（GEMM）操作，无法满足实际实时需求
AI.method: 提出GEMM-GS方法，将3DGS混合过程等价地重新表述为GEMM兼容形式以利用张量核心，设计高性能CUDA内核，集成三阶段双缓冲流水线来重叠计算和内存访问
AI.result: 实验显示GEMM-GS比原始3DGS快1.42倍，与现有加速方法结合时平均额外提供1.47倍加速
AI.conclusion: 通过利用GPU张量核心的GEMM-GS方法能显著加速3D高斯泼溅渲染，满足实时应用需求
